description: KST Evergreen (Pvt) Ltd Vs. Hon. K.D.Lal Kantha and Others. KST Evergreen (Pvt) Ltd Vs. Hon. K.D.Lal Kantha and Others. View More before Hon. K. M. S. Dissanayake, J.
parties: KST Evergreen (Pvt) Ltd Vs. Hon. K.D.Lal Kantha and Others. KST Evergreen (Pvt) Ltd Vs. Hon. K.D.Lal Kantha and Others. View More
judgement_by: Hon. K. M. S. Dissanayake, J.
keywords: Application for Writs under Article 140 of the Constitution – Preliminary legal objections with regard to the validity of the statement of objection and supporting affidavit – Legal effect of Rule 3(7) read with Rule 3(4)(b)(i) of the Court of Appeal (Appellate Procedure) Rules 1990 –Interpretation of the phrase “shall be” occurred in the Rule3(7) of the Court of Appeal (Appellate Procedure) Rules 1990 read with Rule 3(4)(b)(i) thereof –Whether under the circumstances Court has discretion to permit amended statement of objection – What does the phrase “shall be” denote?-Is it mandatory or not?-Requirement that each respondent file statement of objections supported by affidavit – Whether non-compliance with mandatory procedural rules is fatal or curable – Applicability of section 9 of the Oaths and Affirmation Ordinance– Sections 181 and 183A of the Civil Procedure Code – Requirement that the declarant must affirm matters of fact from the deponent’s personal knowledge – Hearsay evidence in affidavits
legistation: Constitution - Article 140, Civil Procedure Code- Sections 180 and 183A, and Rule 3(7) read with Rule 3(4)(b)(i) of the Court of Appeal (Appellate Procedure) Rules 1990.
